channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources

item.title: 🇱🇧 Hezbollah says Lebanon must be included in any US-Iran peace deal
item.description: A senior Hezbollah politician said the group is confident Iran will insist on Lebanon being part of any agreement with the United States, as hopes grow for a deal between Tehran and Washington.

item.title: 🇦🇪 UAE agrees to unlock billions of dollars for Iran, sources say
item.description: The United Arab Emirates has agreed to unlock between billion and billion for Iran, sources told Reuters, in a tactical shift after weeks of Iranian attacks on the Gulf state during the US-Israeli war with Iran.

item.title: 🥶 Antarctica missing sea ice area size of France as temperatures soar 20C above average
item.description: A vast area of the Bellingshausen Sea on Antarctica west coast should be covered by sea ice but is missing, with experts calling the loss unprecedented and temperatures peaking 20 degrees above average.

item.title: 🇵🇭 Philippines earthquake kills at least 55 on Mindanao island
item.description: A powerful earthquake struck Mindanao in the Philippines, killing at least 55 people and leaving a trail of destruction as the country picks up the pieces from its strongest quake in decades.

item.title: 📈 European shares rally as Middle East peace hopes push oil lower
item.description: European stocks surged more than 1% as renewed hopes for a diplomatic breakthrough in the Middle East dragged oil prices lower and injected fresh optimism into markets.

item.title: 🇻🇪🇺🇸⚔️ US strike kills Tren de Aragua leader with Venezuela cooperation
item.description: President Trump announced a "swift and lethal" US military strike killed Hector Rusthenford Guerrero Flores, leader of the Tren de Aragua gang, with Venezuelan assistance. The operation marks unprecedented cooperation between Washington and Caracas.

item.title: 🇮🇷🇺🇸🇮🇱 Iran ceasefire limbo leaves world between peace and war
item.description: Two months after a nominal cease-fire, Iran, Israel and the US remain in a dangerous stalemate with low-intensity clashes continuing. The New York Times examines the precarious state of negotiations and regional tensions.

item.title: 🇺🇦💧 Ukrainian strike cuts water to Russian-held Donetsk
item.description: A Ukrainian military strike disrupted water supplies to parts of the Russian-occupied Donetsk region, including the regional capital, as infrastructure targeting continues on both sides of the war.

item.title: 🚀💰 SpaceX $2 trillion debut divides markets amid cheers and protests
item.description: Elon Musk's SpaceX soared 11% on its first day of trading, reaching a $2 trillion valuation. The record IPO drew both celebration and criticism from investors concerned about valuation and Musk's influence.

item.title: 🇧🇷⚠️ Brazil refineries linked to terrorist financing scheme
item.description: Brazilian refineries sold over 100 million liters of naphtha to a firm under investigation for fraud involving a criminal gang designated by the US as a terrorist organization, Reuters reports.

item.title: 🇭🇰🚁 Hong Kong Tests Heavy-Duty Drones for Construction in Low-Altitude Economy Push
item.description: Hong Kong is testing drones capable of carrying 150kg loads of cement and bricks as part of the government's initiative to develop policies for a low-altitude economy and modernize construction logistics.

item.title: 🇨🇳🚬 China's World's Largest Smoking Population Raises Economic Concerns
item.description: Public health experts warn that China's low taxes on cigarettes risk undermining Beijing's pursuit of a healthier population and could have broader economic implications for the world's second-largest economy.

item.title: 🇺🇸🏛️ Trump Asks Republicans to Formally Erase His Impeachments from Historical Record
item.description: President Donald Trump is urging Republicans in Congress to pass a resolution that would symbolically erase his two impeachments from the congressional record, marking an unprecedented effort to rewrite legislative history.

item.title: 🇷🇺 Putin Announces Satellite-Based Drone Control System for Russian Military
item.description: President Vladimir Putin revealed Russia is developing satellite-based control systems for combat drones, Interfax reported. The announcement comes amid ongoing hostilities with Ukraine and signals continued military technology investment.

item.title: 🇨🇳 China Confirms Arrest of US Scholar Min Zin on Espionage Charges
item.description: China's foreign ministry confirmed the arrest of Min Zin, a US citizen who leads a Myanmar-focused think tank, on suspicion of spying and endangering national security. The arrest adds to US-China tensions.

item.title: 🇺🇸 US Deports Iranian Pro-Democracy Activist to Central African Republic
item.description: The United States has deported an Iranian pro-democracy activist to the Central African Republic, her lawyer said, describing it as a "super dangerous" transfer to a country with which the activist has no connection.

item.title: 🚀 SpaceX Surges Past $2 Trillion Valuation in Record Nasdaq Debut
item.description: Elon Musk's SpaceX saw shares jump 19% in its Nasdaq debut, pushing the company's value past $2 trillion and making Musk the world's first trillionaire. The IPO raised a record $75 billion.

item.title: 🇰🇵 North Korea condemns US missile sale approval to South Korea
item.description: North Korea's foreign ministry condemned a US decision to approve the sale of advanced air-to-air missiles to South Korea, warning it would worsen tensions on the Korean peninsula.

item.title: 🇪🇺 EU envoys greenlight first phase of membership talks with Ukraine and Moldova
item.description: Ambassadors from the EU's 27 countries agreed to advance membership talks with Ukraine and Moldova, with negotiations set to begin on Monday.

item.title: ⛪ Pope Leo XIV flight grounded in Spain, king offers help
item.description: Pope Leo XIV faced an unexpected travel delay when his charter flight back to Rome was grounded due to a technical issue. Spain's King Felipe offered assistance.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Trump and Iran say agreement to end war is close
item.description: President Trump said the US may attend a signing ceremony within days, while Iranian officials denied any final agreement had been reached. A senior US official said both sides agreed on a text.

item.title: 🇮🇷🇺🇸 US and Iran Near Deal to Reopen Strait of Hormuz
item.description: A US-Iran agreement to reopen the strategic Strait of Hormuz could be signed during next week's G7 summit, with both sides expected to extend their ceasefire by approximately two months.

item.title: 🇦🇪🇮🇷 UAE Denies $3 Billion Transfer to Iran
item.description: The UAE categorically denied reports that it had transferred $3 billion to Iran as part of a deal to end attacks on the Gulf country, amid ongoing diplomatic negotiations.

item.title: 💥🇮🇷 Explosion Reported in Southern Iranian Port City of Sirik
item.description: Explosions were reported late Friday in the area of Sirik port in southern Iran, according to Iranian state television, in a region near the strategic Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: 🇬🇧🇷🇺 UK Sets 2027 Deadline for Ban on Russian-Derived Fuel
item.description: The UK announced it will fully phase in its ban on diesel and jet fuel produced from Russian crude oil by 2027, tightening sanctions on Moscow's energy exports.
